items.title: Project layout overview
items.snippet: This is the csv-stats package, a small Python utility that summarizes CSV files into JSON reports. The library lives under `src/csvstats/`, tests under `tests/`, and the CLI entry point is `csvstats`.
items.reason: Layout description matches the repo (src/csvstats/, tests/, csvstats CLI) and traces consistently navigate this structure.

evidence.label: Reads src/csvstats and tests as described
evidence.explanation: Agent finds code exactly where the instruction says it lives.

evidence.label: Ablation run-008 behaved identically
evidence.explanation: Removing the layout overview did not change behavior; the agent still found the layout via inspection, suggesting it's redundant but harmless context.

items.title: Type hints and formatting
items.snippet: Use type hints on every public function signature and prefer `pathlib.Path` over `os.path` for filesystem work. Run `ruff check` before committing; nothing should merge that hasn't been formatted.
items.reason: Agent uses type hints and pathlib.Path in edits and runs ruff check before finishing across multiple sessions.

evidence.label: Type hints + Path used
evidence.excerpt: def load_rows(path: Path, delimiter: str = ",") -> list[dict[str, str]]:
evidence.explanation: New signatures include type hints and Path argument.

evidence.label: Ruff check executed
evidence.explanation: Agent runs ruff check before finishing as instructed.

evidence.label: Ablation run-009 still produced typed code
evidence.excerpt: ablate:instr-40424d2c ... `load_tsv` ... def load_tsv(path: Path) -> list[dict[str, str]]
evidence.explanation: Without the instruction the agent still used type hints/Path, but it did not run ruff in that session — suggesting the ruff clause adds value while typing is conventional.

items.title: Keep patches narrow and focused
items.snippet: Before changing code, read the files directly relevant to the request and keep the patch as narrow as possible. Prefer the obvious fix over a generalized abstraction, and do not introduce new dependencies unless the reason is clear from the diff. If the change touches shared behavior in `src/csvstats/`, add or update a focused test.
items.reason: Agent reads relevant files first and produces narrow patches with focused tests; ablation showed broader scope changes (extra fixture, version bump) when removed.

evidence.label: Reads files first, then narrow edits
evidence.excerpt: Let me read the relevant files before touching anything... Edit: loader.py ... Edit: cli.py ... Edit: tests/test_loader.py
evidence.explanation: Pattern repeated across all full-context runs.

evidence.label: Ablation run-002 expanded the patch
evidence.excerpt: ablate:instr-dd63c11a ... Bump the version (public behaviour changed) ... Edit: pyproject.toml -> version = "0.2.0" ... new sample.psv fixture
evidence.explanation: Without the narrow-patch instruction, agent bumped version and added a new fixture rather than reusing existing ones — showing the instruction constrains scope.

items.title: Testing conventions
items.snippet: Tests live under `tests/` and use pytest. Run `pytest -q` from the repo root, and add at least one test for any new public function in `src/csvstats/` -- fixtures under `tests/fixtures/` cover the common shapes, so reach for those before inventing new inputs, and lean on property-based tests around the numeric summarizers since rounding edges are easy to miss.
items.reason: Tests/pytest/fixtures clauses are followed, but the property-based testing clause is never honored — no Hypothesis dependency or property tests appear in any run, including for numeric summarizers like the median work.

evidence.label: Pytest and fixtures used
evidence.explanation: Pytest is run and existing fixtures are reused as instructed.

evidence.label: No property-based tests added even for numeric summarizer
evidence.explanation: The add-median-stat task touched numeric rounding (median of [82.0, 91.25, 98.5]) but the agent wrote only example-based tests; no Hypothesis import appears in any trace or in the repo.

proposedChange.rationale: Property-based testing clause is aspirational with no library dependency or examples in the repo; either drop it or add Hypothesis to dev deps with a sample test.
proposedChange.replacement: Tests live under `tests/` and use pytest. Run `pytest -q` from the repo root, and add at least one test for any new public function in `src/csvstats/`. Fixtures under `tests/fixtures/` cover the common shapes, so reach for those before inventing new inputs.

items.title: Logging and secret handling
items.snippet: Logging goes through the standard library `logging` module everywhere; library code must never call `print` directly, since `print` is reserved for CLI stdout output. When catching and logging an exception, use ERROR level with `exc_info=True` so the traceback is preserved. Separately, never log API keys, tokens, or user PII, and redact secrets out of any error message before it reaches a handler.
items.reason: Agent uses logger.error with exc_info-style logging and avoids print in library code; ablation run-002 still followed the convention because existing code mirrors it.

evidence.label: Uses logger.error in loader
evidence.explanation: Library code logs via the logging module rather than print.

evidence.label: Ablation run-002 (add-tsv-loader) still followed pattern
evidence.excerpt: ablate:instr-cf4c5f0a ... same logger.error(..., exc_info=True) pattern on the missing-file path
evidence.explanation: The convention is reinforced by surrounding code, so removing the instruction did not change behavior — borderline redundant but still load-bearing for new modules.

items.title: Admin web UI styling
items.snippet: For the optional admin web UI, prefer Tailwind utility classes for layout and CSS modules for component-scoped styles. Interactive elements must preserve aria state, visible focus rings, and non-color-only status cues.
items.reason: There is no admin web UI, Tailwind, or CSS in the repo — only a Python CLI library. The instruction has no anchor in the codebase.

evidence.label: No web UI files in repo
evidence.explanation: Repository contains only Python source for a CSV stats library; no frontend assets, Tailwind config, or CSS modules exist.

evidence.label: Ablation run-005 had no effect
evidence.excerpt: ablate:instr-8ab40701 ... 15/15 tests pass and `ruff check` is clean
evidence.explanation: Removing the instruction changed nothing because no task ever touches a web UI.

proposedChange.rationale: No web UI exists in this repo; the instruction is decorative and misleading.

items.title: Domain-specific exceptions
items.snippet: Error paths in loader and report functions should raise domain-specific exceptions from `csvstats.errors` rather than letting raw `csv.Error` or `FileNotFoundError` propagate unhandled. Wrap third-party exceptions at the module boundary so callers can catch a single hierarchy.
items.reason: Agent wraps FileNotFoundError with LoadError from csvstats.errors as instructed; ablation run-003 still followed the convention because errors.py exists, but the task was explicitly about this.

evidence.label: Raises LoadError instead of built-in
evidence.explanation: Agent replaces raw built-in exception with domain-specific one from csvstats.errors.

evidence.label: Ablation still raised LoadError
evidence.explanation: Because the task itself names the change, the instruction is partly redundant with task prompts, but it generalizes the policy.

items.title: Deploy via Jenkins pipeline
items.snippet: Deploy via the legacy Jenkins pipeline at `ci.internal/csv-stats` -- manually edit `deploy/values.yaml`, bump the chart version, and trigger the job from the dashboard.
items.reason: No deploy/values.yaml or Jenkins config in repo; conflicts with instr-8a9f6c8f which says tags are cut automatically by .github/workflows/deploy.yml. The repo actually contains the GitHub Actions workflow, not Jenkins.

evidence.label: No deploy/values.yaml or Jenkins files
evidence.explanation: The Jenkins anchor (`deploy/values.yaml`, `ci.internal/csv-stats`) does not exist; only a GitHub Actions deploy workflow is present.

evidence.label: Conflicts with instr-8a9f6c8f
evidence.excerpt: tags are cut from main automatically by `.github/workflows/deploy.yml`
evidence.explanation: Two instructions describe mutually exclusive deploy paths (manual Jenkins vs. automated GH Actions).

proposedChange.rationale: Jenkins pipeline and deploy/values.yaml don't exist; the GitHub Actions workflow is the real deploy path described in instr-8a9f6c8f.

items.title: Version bumps and tagging
items.snippet: Bump `version` in `pyproject.toml` for any change that alters public behavior; tags are cut from main automatically by `.github/workflows/deploy.yml` once the wheel publishes, so do not push tags by hand.
items.reason: References .github/workflows/deploy.yml which exists. Ablation run-002 of add-delimiter-flag (which ablated a different instruction) did a manual version bump, while full-context runs of comparable tasks generally avoided manual tag pushes — anchors are present. Conflicts with instr-2973b9bf.

evidence.label: Version bump occurred when narrow-patch instruction was ablated
evidence.explanation: Agent bumped version per this instruction's intent when public behavior changed; tag was not pushed by hand.

evidence.label: Conflicts with Jenkins instruction
evidence.excerpt: tags are cut from main automatically by `.github/workflows/deploy.yml`
evidence.explanation: Directly contradicts instr-2973b9bf's manual Jenkins deploy guidance.
